1. Fetching FastF1 Data
	- Create Dataframes [Cody]
	- Identify Location of races and times
2. Fetching OpenMeteo Data
	- Using location (lat, long) and time get weather conditions [Luis]
3. Defining what's considered a "chaotic" weather conditions
	- Defined categories (Wet, Hot, Windy) [Vamsi]
	- Group weather conditions with race locations [Vamsi]
	- Create a subset of races to analyze data from
	- Combine FastF1 weather (collected by minute) conditions along with OpenMeteo's (hourly combined) data
4. Create a rank using basic statistics (median)
	- Rank drivers based on starting position and finishing position (positions gained/lost) [Vamsi]
	- Observe consitency of driver's performance during the race, per lap-time, using FastF1's weather data
5. Analysis lap-time performance in adverse weather (In different weather conditions)
	- Visualizations
6. Analysis on team perfomance (In different weather conditions)
	- Visualizations
7. Analysis comparing the drivers of the same team
